events.title: World's worst energy crisis? Iran war sparks scramble for alternatives to Gulf oil
events.description: Will the world have enough fuel to fill the tank and fertilize the crops? The seizure of ships by Iran adding to global jitters as the extension of a ceasefire offers zero visibility on prospects for the planet’s biggest choke point for oil and gas. Nearly two months on, the Paris-based International Energy Agency is calling it the biggest-ever energy disruption in history. We’ll ask about prices at the pump, cancelled flights, and…more broadly, if oil's not flowing through the Strait of Hormuz, where to find it and what’s the alternative?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World's worst energy crisis? Iran war sparks scramble for alternatives to Gulf oil

events.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
events.description: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

events.title: Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn
events.description: Extreme heat is pushing global food and farming systems to the brink, threatening the livelihoods of over a billion people as rising temperatures and more frequent heatwaves redefine how food is produced worldwide, a new UN report warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn

events.title: Massive 7.7-magnitude earthquake hits off Japanese coast
events.description: A strong earthquake erupted off the coast of the nation of Japan, the Associated Press reported, noting the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center indicated the tsunami threat "has now passed."
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Massive 7.7-magnitude earthquake hits off Japanese coast

events.title: Who is Ahmad Vahidi? Iran’s new IRGC chief tied to global attacks and ‘Death to America’ ideology
events.description: Shadowy IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi is emerging as one of Iran's most powerful figures as Trump's ceasefire deadline approaches, analysts warn.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Who is Ahmad Vahidi? Iran’s new IRGC chief tied to global attacks and ‘Death to America’ ideology

events.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
events.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike

events.title: Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages
events.description: Three years into the devastating conflict in Sudan, nearly four million displaced people have returned to their places of origin across the country, only to face “another struggle for survival”, the UN International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages

events.title: ‘We were terrified they were going to kill us’: fishers who survived US boat strike speak out
events.description: An Ecuadorian fishing crew describe their ordeal as victims of Trump’s purported war on ‘narcoterrorists’ By 4pm, the light was softening over the Pacific, and the crew of the Don Maca were finishing a long day hauling in lines of swordfish and albacore. Down in the hold, the mood had settled into the familiar rhythm of a fishing day drawing to a close. “We were just working, waiting for the last trawler to return,” said Jhonny Sebastián Palacios, one of the fishers. “Everything was perfectly fine.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘We were terrified they were going to kill us’: fishers who survived US boat strike speak out

events.title: Thirteen killed in second India fireworks blast in three days
events.description: Earlier this week, an explosion at a firecracker factory in neighbouring state Tamil Nadu left at least 25 dead.

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks
events.description: The Security Council meets in emergency session at 3pm to address the escalating conflict in Ukraine. The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments, including devastating strikes on the city of Dnipro. Ukraine has reported that between late March and mid-April, more than 5,000 drones and missiles were launched, killing dozens of civilians and injuring hundreds more. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users can follow here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks

events.title: Trial opens after Nigeria charges six over 2025 coup plot
events.description: In tonight's edition, in Nigeria, six men are charged with planning an attack on the presidential villa. Also, a new investigation suggests foreign fighters, including Colombians, have been backing one side in the Sudan conflict. And we take a dive into the art world with the latest exhibition from the Nigerian-born artist Eniwaye Oluwasey in Paris.

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition
events.description: The Security Council is meeting now at a critical juncture for Syria’s fragile transition. While the Iran-US ceasefire has temporarily eased regional spillover from Lebanon and elsewhere, conditions remain volatile in Syria. The reported US military withdrawal now completed, marks a milestone, yet the chamber faces a country grappling with deep internal fissures and external pressures. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users should click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition

events.title: Iran war: Standoff at Hormuz casts shadow over Iran ceasefire talks
events.description: The prospects for renewed peace talks wavered after Iran seized two international vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Standoff at Hormuz casts shadow over Iran ceasefire talks

events.title: Timor-Leste parliament questions president over proposed resort’s links to ‘scam’ empire
events.description: Jose Ramos-Horta urged by opposition to explain diplomatic passport given to businessman behind resort project, who denies any involvement with organised crime Timor-Leste’s opposition has questioned how foreign investors in a proposed cryptocurrency resort obtained prime beachfront real estate in the country’s capital, and has called on the president to explain why he issued a diplomatic passport to a Chinese businessman involved in the project. Speaking in parliament in Dili on Monday, Fretilin opposition party MP Florentino Ximenes da Costa “Sinarai” raised concerns about the proposed AB Digital Technology Resort, which was the subject of a months-long investigation by the Guardian and Organised Crime and Corruption Reporting Project (OCCRP) . Continue reading...

events.title: Long hours, stress and harassment are causing hundreds of thousands of early deaths, says UN labour agency
events.description: More than 840,000 people die each year from health conditions linked to risks such as long working hours, job insecurity, workplace harassment and bullying, according to a new report by the International Labour Organization (ILO).

events.title: Child reunited with mom after transgender dad, partner allegedly kidnap abroad amid gender surgery fears: feds
events.description: Federal authorities allege a Utah child was taken to Cuba during a planned camping trip, sparking an international parental kidnapping investigation.

events.title: Los Angeles becomes first major US school district to limit classroom screen time
events.description: The school district, which serves about half a million students, is looking to reduce its reliance on devices after the pandemic.

events.title: Israel kills journalist and wounds another in south Lebanon targeted attack
events.description: Israel has killed journalist Amal Khalil and injured Zeinab Faraj in a ‘double-tap’ attack in southern Lebanon.

events.title: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanese journalist; Tehran-US talks stalled
events.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon killed at least five people on Wednesday, including Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanese journalist; Tehran-US talks stalled

events.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
events.description: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang

events.title: NASA nuclear engineer found dead in burned Tesla after vanishing from his Alabama home last year
events.description: NASA nuclear propulsion engineer Joshua LeBlanc died in a mysterious fiery Tesla crash in Alabama, becoming the 12th scientist to die or go missing.
events.ticker_label: 💥 NASA nuclear engineer found dead in burned Tesla after vanishing from his Alabama home last year

events.title: Mexico pyramid shooter who took hostages and killed 1 is identified
events.description: Officials identified the gunman who fatally shot a Canadian tourist and wounded others atop a historic pyramid at Teotihuacan in Mexico on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mexico pyramid shooter who took hostages and killed 1 is identified

events.title: Mysterious death of Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay referred to homicide squad
events.description: Melbourne man stabbed twice in chest, with remains found in field near his campervan in 2021. Family argue police prematurely assumed he’d killed himself Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales coroner has recommended the mysterious death of Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay be investigated by the homicide squad, with Teresa O’Sullivan finding “some aspects of the early [police] investigation raised concerns”. Stacker had been travelling from Melbourne to Queensland in his Toyota Hiace van in 2021 when the borders closed due to Covid. Continue reading...

events.title: UK’s elite soldier unit faces exodus over ‘lawfare’ fears — warning sign for US military?
events.description: Britain's elite SAS faces a growing exodus as soldiers resign over fears of legal scrutiny for actions in Afghanistan and other conflicts, report says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK’s elite soldier unit faces exodus over ‘lawfare’ fears — warning sign for US military?

events.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years
events.description: There are now 3,110 billionaires but analysis shows ‘deep structural acceleration’ in wealth creation around world The number of billionaires in the world could reach nearly 4,000 by 2031, figures suggest, as the super-rich accumulate wealth at an accelerating rate. There are now 3,110 billionaires globally, according to analysis by the estate agent Knight Frank. This is forecast to rise by 25% over the next five years, taking the total to 3,915. Continue reading...

events.title: Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids
events.description: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids

events.title: Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids
events.description: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids

events.title: Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The United States’ decision to extend a fragile ceasefire with Iran has kept a narrow window open for diplomacy, but fresh security incidents in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday underscore the volatility of the situation and the risks to global shipping and regional stability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Nature’s last strongholds: Why UNESCO sites are a lifeline for a planet in peril
events.description: In a world where the drumbeat of environmental loss is growing louder, a new landmark report from UNESCO has revealed a rare glimmer of hope. While global wildlife has plummeted by a staggering 73 per cent since the 1970s, life within the UN science and culture agency’s protected territories is holding its ground.

events.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard sidelines president as military grip expands
events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps effectively has assumed control over key state functions and blocked presidential appointments, according to an Iran International report.

events.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
events.description: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his friends in Sydney after returning to China in December 2024. Continue reading...

events.title: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
events.description: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes

events.title: Three ships targeted in Hormuz, Iran seizes two
events.description: Iranian forces targeted three container ships on Wednesday, seizing two, the latest incidents to threaten a crucial trade route in the Middle East war. Iran's parliament speaker said on Wednesday that the Islamic republic would not reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US naval blockade remained in place, calling it a "blatant violation of the ceasefire". FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three ships targeted in Hormuz, Iran seizes two

events.title: Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal
events.description: Libya’s political leaders continue to lag in implementing a roadmap that leads to national elections and unified institutions, the UN Special Representative for the country told the Security Council on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal

events.title: EU proposes steps to deal with energy crisis amid Iran war
events.description: Brussels has unveiled a toolbox to manage energy shocks, as concerns grow over fuel supplies and rising costs. It includes a push to decrease the bloc's reliance on fossil fuels.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU proposes steps to deal with energy crisis amid Iran war

events.title: 'Right now both sides are testing each other', analyst says as Iran truce extended
events.description: FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Negah Angha, Former National Security Advisor under US President Joe Biden, about the ongoing standoff between Iran and the United States, which has choked off all traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. She says that "right now both sides are testing each other" adding that "Iran has been articulating to the US and Western allies the impact and the control that they have on the global economy".

events.title: Israel accused of war crimes after 'targeted' killing of journalist
events.description: Israel has again been accused of targeting journalists after the death of a reporter working in southern Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel accused of war crimes after 'targeted' killing of journalist

events.title: Deadly shooting at historic tourist site leaves one dead, several injured as motive unclear
events.description: A shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacan pyramids left a Canadian woman dead and multiple tourists injured, according to Mexican officials and security reports.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Deadly shooting at historic tourist site leaves one dead, several injured as motive unclear

events.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
events.description: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex

events.title: How does the war in Iran end?
events.description: 👉Listen to The World with Richard Engel and Yalda Hakim on your podcast app👈
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How does the war in Iran end?

events.title: China weathered Trump's tariffs - but the Iran war is taking a toll
events.description: The Middle East conflict is putting pressure on factory orders, costs and jobs in China's export-driven economy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China weathered Trump's tariffs - but the Iran war is taking a toll

events.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
events.description: Mexico to investigate possible breach of its constitution and assess US’s role in anti-drug operation near Chihuahua Mexico has launched an investigation into a possible breach of its constitution as it was reported that two US embassy officials who died in a car accident while returning from a raid on a drug lab with local officials in the border state of Chihuahua were CIA operatives. The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their vehicle skidded off the road and plunged down a 200 metre ravine in the mountains near Chihuahua’s border with the state of Sinaloa. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents

events.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort
events.description: Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort

events.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
events.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

events.title: Iran war fuels crisis for Africa's aviation sector
events.description: The Iran war is pushing up jet fuel prices, forcing African airlines to raise ticket prices, cancel flights and rethink operations to stay financially viable.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war fuels crisis for Africa's aviation sector

events.title: EU unveils plan to tackle energy crisis
events.description: The European Union has unveiled plans to shield households and businesses from the energy crisis, triggered by the war with Iran. The bloc says it has spent an additional €24 billion on energy imports beacuse of higher prices since the war began, and it urged more investment in renewable energy. Also in the show - French President Emmanuel Macron inaugurates a lithium mine, to reduce reliance on imports of critical minerals.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU unveils plan to tackle energy crisis

events.title: Macron says French peacekeeper wounded in Lebanon attack over the weekend has died
events.description: Macron says French peacekeeper wounded in Lebanon attack over the weekend has died
events.ticker_label: 💥 Macron says French peacekeeper wounded in Lebanon attack over the weekend has died

events.title: Florida man's execution date set for killing 2, including small child
events.description: A Florida man is scheduled for execution by lethal injection next month after Gov. DeSantis signed a death warrant for his 2000 double murder conviction.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Florida man's execution date set for killing 2, including small child

events.title: Workers killed and many treated after refinery chemical release as investigators probe cause
events.description: A chemical release at a West Virginia refinery killed two people and left 21 others needing medical care as emergency crews raced to contain the scene.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
events.description: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

events.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
events.description: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people injured, according to media reports.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 21 April: Talks unclear; Hormuz tensions continue
events.description: Uncertainty surrounds possible US-Iran talks in Pakistan, with no confirmation yet that they will go ahead. At the same time, tensions persist in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over maritime security, while the humanitarian crisis in Lebanon continues despite the ceasefire. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 21 April: Talks unclear; Hormuz tensions continue

events.title: Gaza: Human development set back 77 years as recovery costs rise to $71 billion
events.description: The UN and European Union issued a joint warning on Monday that human development across Gaza has been set back by a staggering 77 years, with $71.4 billion needed over the next decade for recovery and reconstruction.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza: Human development set back 77 years as recovery costs rise to $71 billion

events.title: Former Mexican beauty queen found shot dead as investigators examine possible family involvement: reports
events.description: Prosecutors investigate the death of Carolina Flores Gómez, a former Mexican beauty queen found shot in her Mexico City apartment in the Polanco area.

events.title: Australia news live: Albanese says his mother would have been eligible for the trimmed-down NDIS; Minns says about a third of cuts will be in NSW
events.description: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast James Valentine’s family has released a statement after his death. Here’s what they had to say: James passed peacefully at home surrounded by his family, who adored him. Throughout his illness, James did it his way, which lasted all the way until the end when he made the choice to do Voluntary Assisted Dying. Both he and his family are grateful he was given the option to go out on his own terms. He was calm, dignified as always and somehow still making us laugh. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Australia news live: Albanese says his mother would have been eligible for the trimmed-down NDIS; Minns says about a third of cuts will be in NSW

events.title: Japan: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead
events.description: Police said a South Korean national holding a banner with political messages was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine to Japan's war dead. Japan's prime minister did not attend proceedings, but around 120 lawmakers did.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead

events.title: World News in Brief: Insecurity in the Darfurs, 100 million live with landmine threats, Singapore execution moratorium
events.description: The United Nations is deeply alarmed by continued insecurity in the Darfur region of Sudan and the heavy toll it is having on civilians, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday.

events.title: Pirro warns car theft ring used new tech to quickly access vehicles: 'Gone in 60 seconds'
events.description: A federal indictment alleges a car theft ring used electronic Autel devices to steal nearly $1 million in vehicles and ship them to Africa for resale.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pirro warns car theft ring used new tech to quickly access vehicles: 'Gone in 60 seconds'

events.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
events.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

events.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel

events.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
events.description: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the US The Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday. The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times , come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts to apply to resettle in the US. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo

events.title: Iran says it seized ships in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. blockade continues amid ceasefire
events.description: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran says it seized ships in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. blockade continues amid ceasefire

events.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
events.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back

events.title: Iran war: Revolutionary Guard says seizes ships in Hormuz
events.description: Iran's naval forces accused two ships of endangering maritime security by attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz without authorization. The escalation comes amid uncertainty over US-Iran ceasefire talks. DW has more.

events.title: Middle East war live: Lebanon meets Israel in Washington to request truce extension
events.description: Israel and Lebanon hold a new round of talks in Washington on Thursday, during which Beirut plans to request a one-month extension of a ceasefire due to expire within days. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war live: Lebanon meets Israel in Washington to request truce extension

events.title: UN chief candidates vow reforms to restore trust, revive global role
events.description: Candidates for the top job at the United Nations pledged on Wednesday to pursue reforms to revive the body, while reaffirming its core role in peacemaking and development. Four contenders aim to succeed Antonio Guterres next year, following hearings with member states and civil society.

events.title: Sheinbaum weighs sanctions on Chihuahua state after CIA agents died after drug lab raid in Mexico
events.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday that she's considering possible sanctions against the government of Chihuahua — a state bordering Texas — for allowing CIA agents to participate in an operation to dismantle drug laboratories, because any security collaboration with the U.S. should be approved by Mexico's federal government. FRANCE 24's Ioan Grillo reports from Mexico City.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Sheinbaum weighs sanctions on Chihuahua state after CIA agents died after drug lab raid in Mexico

events.title: Pope tells prisoners in Equatorial Guinea 'you are not alone'

events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Pope tells prisoners in Equatorial Guinea 'you are not alone'

events.title: Owner of gun used to kill eight children says it was stolen from his truck in weeks prior

events.title: 2 trains collide in Denmark, prompting a massive emergency response north of Copenhagen
events.description: Two trains collided in Denmark, prompting a massive emergency response for what police called a major accident

events.title: Hiker who set out in warm spring weather found dead after snowstorm in New Hampshire mountains
events.description: A Massachusetts hiker was found dead in New Hampshire's White Mountains after a snowstorm dumped several inches of snow, officials said.

events.title: Mexican president in the dark about drug operation after which US, Mexican officials were killed
events.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum seeks explanations regarding what U.S. and Mexican officials were doing when deadly accident occurred.

events.title: Democrats up in Virginia, but US voters may pay price for redistricting war
events.description: Experts say unprecedented flurry of tit-for-tat congressional redistricting risks surge in gerrymandering.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Democrats up in Virginia, but US voters may pay price for redistricting war

events.title: US suspect accused of stealing Kristi Noem’s purse sentenced to three years
events.description: President Trump has used incidents of crime to justify ongoing National Guard deployment to the capital, Washington, DC.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Pentagon removes top Navy official amid military shake-up
events.description: US Secretary of the Navy John Phelan has left his post with immediate effect, the Pentagon said Wednesday, without providing an explanation. His departure comes amid a broader clear-out of senior military figures under President Donald Trump, as the United States remains locked in an unresolved conflict with Iran.

events.title: Gulf allies have requested financial support, US Treasury Secretary says
events.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Wednesday that a number of allies in the Gulf region and ​in Asia have ‌requested currency swap lines from the United States to help deal with energy shocks ⁠and other fallout from the Middle East war. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Dr Kristi Diwan, Senior Resident Scholar at the Arab Gulf States Institute.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf allies have requested financial support, US Treasury Secretary says

events.title: Four people critically injured after two trains collide
events.description: Four people have been left in a critical condition after two trains collided in Denmark, local reports say.

events.title: Voting begins in India's West Bengal state after a national voter list purge
events.description: Voting in one of India’s most closely watched state elections has begun after a national voter roll revision removed millions of names and raised concerns over disenfranchisement

events.title: Alleged coup plotters in Nigeria arraigned on charges of treason and terrorism
events.description: Six people accused of plotting to overthrow Nigerian President Bola Tinubu have been arraigned in court on charges of treason and terrorism

events.title: Tokyo theme park worker dies after being trapped inside ride mechanism during routine maintenance
events.description: An employee conducting routine maintenance at Tokyo Dome City Attractions reportedly died after becoming trapped in the "Flying Balloon" ride mechanism.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Tokyo theme park worker dies after being trapped inside ride mechanism during routine maintenance

events.title: Pope Leo urges Africans to stay and 'serve your country' instead of migrating as displacement climbs

events.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Carney names broad team to advise on tense US-Canada trade talks
events.description: Conservatives and former provincial premiers among those PM names to advisory committee on economic relations Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney , says his new advisory committee on economic relations with the United States will draw on the “best advice and the broadest perspectives” as the country braces for what many expect will be tense trade negotiations with its southern neighbour. The 24-member advisory committee, announced on Tuesday, shows the prime minister’s eagerness to reach across the political spectrum to ensure Canada is “well positioned to advance its interests” at the looming trade talks. Continue reading...

events.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
events.description: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

events.title: Nepal: Interior minister elected on anti-corruption platform steps down amid financial investigation
events.description: Nepal's new government elected on an anti-corruption platform after youth protests toppled its predecessor has now lost two ministers in weeks. Sudan Gurung said he was quitting to ensure "a fair investigation."

events.title: World News in Brief: Tackling the AI digital divide, deadly migration journeys, Lucy Hale named new WFP Goodwill Ambassador
events.description: UN-led efforts are continuing to ensure that all countries can benefit and regulate Artificial Intelligence (AI) as it increasingly shapes our economies, societies and daily lives.

events.description: New data shows that nearly three in four countries in Europe now use Artificial Intelligence in their health services to make a diagnosis.

events.title: Myrtle Beach man with lengthy arrest record charged with stabbing two people in popular beach destination
events.description: Police in Myrtle Beach say a stabbing in the popular beach town left two people injured. Jeffrey Keitt, 68, faces aggravated assault and weapons charges.

events.title: Yamal scores but injured as Barcelona beat Celta to restore La Liga lead

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: French Cup: Nice reach final after victory at Strasbourg
events.description: Nice secured a 2–0 win away at Strasbourg in the French Cup semi-final and will face Lens in the final. Elye Wahi made the difference with a brace. Initially dominated by a Strasbourg side sitting 8th in Ligue 1, Nice, 15th, remained defensively solid and clinically efficient in attack. They will return to the Stade de France on 22 May, four years after losing their previous final to Nantes (1–0).

events.title: Pope Leo condemns inmate conditions on tour of infamous Equatorial Guinea prison
events.description: Pope Leo XIV visited a notorious prison in Bata on the last leg of his Africa tour Wednesday, delivering a rare public critique of inmate conditions in Equatorial Guinea. In his address, the pontiff urged respect for human dignity, highlighting concerns about overcrowding and justice in a country long accused of rights abuses.

events.title: ‘Hungry all the time’: US Navy deny food shortages after grim photos emerge
events.description: The US Navy has fiercely denied claims that its warships in the Middle East are running low on food and leaving its sailors “hungry all the time,” as Washington’s blockade in the Strait of Hormuz continues. Photos showing dismal, mostly-empty trays of food circulated online, with the claims that shortages are rife after a month and a half of war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Hungry all the time’: US Navy deny food shortages after grim photos emerge

events.title: French soldier dies of wounds after attack on UN force in Lebanon
events.description: President Emmanuel Macron said on Wednesday that a second French soldier had died following an attack on United Nations peacekeepers in Lebanon last week, ​which ‌he said was carried out by Iran-backed Hezbollah. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with the spokesperson of the UN peacekeeping mission UNIFIL, Tilak Pokharel.
events.ticker_label: 💥 French soldier dies of wounds after attack on UN force in Lebanon

events.title: The deadly drug raid fuelling speculation about what the US is doing in Mexico
events.description: The deaths of two US officials in a counter-narcotics operation in northern Mexico have sparked uncertainty over the extent to which the Mexican government knew about Washington's involvement.

events.title: Marine veteran allegedly gunned down by husband in attack outside home, police say

events.title: Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force
events.description: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilots involved had been suspended from flying duties, received severe disciplinary action and has since left the military. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force

events.title: Queensland police recall 15,000 service-issued Glock handguns after discovery of fault causing multiple shots to fire
events.description: A malfunction was discovered during routine testing of the firearm, which is standard issue for QPS officers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Queensland police have been forced to retest all service-issued Glock handguns after a fault was discovered in the weapon that could cause it to fire multiple shots. The 40-calibre Glock is the standard-issue sidearm for Queensland police officers, and has been in service since the 1990s. Fifteen thousand weapons will be recalled for testing. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran reportedly fires on three ships in Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: George Ariyoshi, first US governor of Asian American descent, dies aged 100
events.description: Democrat led Hawaii from 1973 to 1986, coinciding with the party’s rise to power in the state George R Ariyoshi – Hawaii’s former governor and the nation’s first Asian American governor – has died at age 100. Ariyoshi, a Democrat who led the state from 1973 to 1986, died peacefully while surrounded by family on Sunday night, according to a statement Monday from the current governor, Josh Green. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 George Ariyoshi, first US governor of Asian American descent, dies aged 100

events.title: Kyle Sandilands berated Kiis FM listeners and bosses in expletive-laden rants, court documents claim
events.description: Radio company ARN’s defence alleges the shock jock denigrated executives, censors, critics and the audience of the Kyle and Jackie O Show Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Sign up for Guardian Australia’s free weekly media newsletter here Kyle Sandilands repeatedly berated Kiis FM executives, the radio station’s censors, critics of the Kyle and Jackie O Show and the Melbourne audience in expletive-laden rants, according to federal court documents. The details of the alleged tirades, which aired months before the shock jock was sacked and accused of “serious misconduct” by ARN, are contained in the company’s defence of an $85m claim filed by Sandilands. Sign up to get Guardian Australia’s weekly media diary as a free newsletter Continue reading...

events.title: Pope Leo decries inequality in corrupt Equatorial Guinea

events.title: G7 omits climate change from Paris talks to avoid US clash, France says
events.description: A meeting of the G7 nations on the environment begins in Paris on Thursday, but climate change has been left off the agenda to avoid a dispute with the United States, France’s ecology minister Monique Barbut said, citing efforts to preserve unity and focus on less contentious issues during the two-day talks.

events.title: As Colombia elections near, Security Council hears calls for calm
events.description: While hope for peace is alive in Colombia as the country prepares for presidential elections, the Security Council on Tuesday heard that achieving it – and making it last – still requires the full implementation of a peace accord signed almost 10 years ago.

events.title: British woman died in Ghana trying to recoup money from scammers, inquest told
events.description: Janet Fordham died in crash after travelling to see man who claimed he would help to recover money from earlier scams A British woman who was scammed out of up to £1m in a string of so-called romance frauds died in a road crash after travelling to west Africa to try to recoup some of her lost fortune, an inquest in Devon has heard. Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inquest in Exeter was told. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request

events.title: Zelensky to join European leaders in Cyprus as EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan
events.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky is scheduled to meet with EU leaders Thursday in Cyprus as the bloc prepares to approve a €90 billion loan for Ukraine, ending a prolonged deadlock. Hungary premier Viktor Orban had vetoed the funds until Kyiv repaired a pipeline hit by a Russian strike – work now completed, allowing oil flows to resume – shortly after his electoral defeat to pro-EU rival Peter Magyar.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelensky to join European leaders in Cyprus as EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan

events.title: Earth Day: The battle to save a drowning Pacific island nation
events.description: As the Pacific island nation of Tuvalu battles rising sea levels that threaten to put large parts of the island entirely underwater by the end of this century, its citizens are making efforts to safeguard their future while preparing for the worst impacts of climate change.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
events.description: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

events.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
events.description: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

events.title: Trump claims ‘rigged’ Virginia vote as court moves to block map redraw
events.description: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday alleged without evidence that a Virginia vote approving a congressional redistricting plan favouring Democrats was “rigged”, as a county judge moved to block the measure. Voters backed the referendum on Tuesday, a change that could help Democrats flip up to four Republican-held House seats.

events.title: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
events.description: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah
events.description: A second French soldier died on Wednesday from wounds suffered in a weekend ambush against UN peacekeepers in Lebanon blamed on Hezbollah, President Emmanuel Macron said. A first soldier, staff sergeant Florian Montorio, was shot dead in the Saturday ambush, for which Hezbollah has denied responsibility. FRANCE 24's Camille Corcoran reports.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. events.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip

Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

events.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments
events.description: The former ballet dancer said Chalamet’s comments were ‘reckless’ in an interview with the New York Times in which she also discussed her violent childhood Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera . In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms that we need to lift up constantly because, yes, they do have a hard time. But in 10 years, AI is going to be able to do Timothée’s job, but it will not be able to replace a person on a stage dancing live.” Continue reading...

events.description: Polling has begun in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal in high-stakes assembly elections. DW has more.

events.title: Palantir: Why its political manifesto is causing a stir
events.description: The US tech company has posted a 22-point manifesto on X that goes well beyond typical corporate communications. Critics say it is evidence of "technofascism."

events.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
events.description: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

events.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
events.description: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting

events.title: ICC blocks ex-Philippine President Duterte release, says it can try him
events.description: Judges ruled the court has authority to try the former Philippine leader despite the Philippines' exit from the ICC.

events.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows on key pipeline supplying Berlin
events.description: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

events.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
events.description: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

events.title: UK, France agree on three-year deal to curb Channel migrant crossings
events.description: Britain and France agreed to a three-year plan to curb irregular Channel crossings, a French interior ministry roadmap showed on Wednesday. France will boost coastal policing by over 50 percent to 1,400 officers by 2029, while Britain will provide up to €766 million in funding, partly conditional on results.

events.title: Equatorial Guinea: Pope denounces detention conditions
events.description: In Equatorial Guinea, Pope Leo XIV called for greater freedoms during a mass attended by nearly 100,000 worshippers.

events.title: The robot that can outplay elite table tennis players
events.description: A robot has become so good at playing table tennis that at times it defeated elite human players, in what has been hailed as "a longstanding milestone for AI".

events.title: British Army uses civilian ferry to take more than 1,400 troops to Europe
events.description: The British Army has used a civilian ferry to send troops to mainland Europe en masse for the first time in a generation.

events.title: The tactic monkeys have adopted to settle stomachs after eating junk food
events.description: Monkeys in Gibraltar have learned to eat soil to settle their stomachs from all the junk food they consume, research suggests.

events.title: Tesla surprises Wall Street and says demand for vehicles has returned
events.description: Tesla defied expectations, profiting more and spending less than expected in closely watched results as the company shifts focus from electric vehicles to artificial intelligence (AI).
events.ticker_label: 💥 Tesla surprises Wall Street and says demand for vehicles has returned

events.title: Canada's prime minister says the US does not get to dictate terms for a trade agreement
events.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says that Washington cannot dictate the terms of the continental trade deal known as USMCA

events.title: UK passes bill that will eventually ban cigarette purchases
events.description: Parliament has passed a bill to make cigarettes inaccessible to future generations in the U.K. Children born after Dec. 31, 2008, will never be able to buy cigarettes under the new Tobacco and Vapes Bill

events.title: China's foreign and defense ministers meet with Cambodian counterparts in joint '2+2' dialogue
events.description: Cambodia and China have held their first meeting of what is called a “2+2” Strategic Dialogue Mechanism

events.title: Russia says its Africa Corps has freed Russian and Ukrainian citizens abducted in Niger
events.description: Russia's Africa Corps has freed a Russian citizen and a Ukrainian abducted by an al-Qaida affiliate in Niger

events.title: Ukraine wants a Zelenskyy-Putin summit to jolt stalled US-led peace efforts
events.description: Kyiv’s top diplomat says Ukraine is pushing for direct talks between President Zelenskyy and Russian President Putin

events.title: EU close to approving release of $106B loan to Ukraine after months of deadlock
events.description: European Union envoys are close to approving a 90 billion euro ($106 billion) loan package to support Ukraine’s military and financial needs for the next two years

events.title: Pope calls for justice and closing income gap in Equatorial Guinea Mass with presidential family
events.description: Pope Leo XIV is urging Equatorial Guineans to work for freedom, justice and to close the gap “between the privileged and the disadvantaged.”

events.title: How 2 men claimed an absurd record by driving an old 3-wheel car the length of Africa
events.description: Two friends have claimed a bizarre record by driving a decades-old British three-wheel car about 14,000 miles from the U.K. to the southern tip of Africa for the longest trip by a three-wheel vehicle

events.title: Time to apply the brakes to runaway AI, says pioneer
events.description: If AI is “a very fast car with no steering wheel” then regulation must provide one, insists Nobel laureate and Artificial Intelligence pioneer Geoffrey Hinton, the visionary scientist widely known as the “godfather” of the self-learning tech.

events.title: Economic collapse pushes highly educated Gazans into the ‘survival economy’
events.description: Young Palestinians in Gaza with university-level educations are setting aside dreams of putting their hard-won skills into practice and doing whatever they can to survive.

events.title: Secretary-General hopefuls make their case in televised ‘interactive dialogues’
events.description: The four candidates so far in the running to be the next UN Secretary-General will each have the chance to show why they are the best choice during a series of “interactive dialogues” taking place at UN Headquarters in New York this week.

events.title: Man who pleaded guilty to raping 12-year-old relative is illegal immigrant from Honduras, DHS says
events.description: Jose Lopez-Montoya, an illegal immigrant from Honduras, pleaded guilty to raping and impregnating a 12-year-old relative and faces up to 99 years in prison.

events.title: Florida prisoner laughs as judge sentences him to life for killing cellmate with pen: 'You are amusing'
events.description: A Florida inmate smiled and laughed in court as a judge sentenced him to life in prison for killing his cellmate by stabbing him in the head with a pen.

events.title: Party prep tale falls apart as husband charged in wife’s decades-old cold case murder
events.description: Authorities say Dwight Rust Jr. is responsible for his wife Michelle Rust's 2002 cold case murder. He faces first-degree murder charges in Maryland.

events.title: Husband of American woman who vanished in the Bahamas hires US lawyer after returning from island
events.description: Brian Hooker has retained a Michigan-based attorney after returning to the U.S. as the search for his wife Lynette Hooker continues in the Bahamas.

events.title: Killer admits to decades-old cold case slaying after investigators lean on new forensic evidence: officials
events.description: Mark Sanfratello pleads guilty and receives 20 years in prison for the 1983 cold case killing of girlfriend Teresa Peroni after a 43-year wait.

events.title: Ex-Alabama girls high school basketball coach charged with ‘deviant’ sex with student, dumped by husband
events.description: Former Alabama girls basketball coach Paige Adams faces over 30 sex crime charges involving a student after a Cullman County grand jury indictment.

events.title: Chicago alderman says police are 'handcuffed' from stopping teen takeovers under Mayor Brandon Johnson
events.description: A Chicago alderman claims police are handcuffed from stopping teen takeovers, blaming Mayor Brandon Johnson's policies for a lack of accountability.

events.title: Trump envoy to Turkey doubles down after backlash, pushes ‘peace through strength’ policy
events.description: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and Syria envoy Tom Barrack defends controversial remarks on Hezbollah, Turkey’s F-35 access and Middle East governance as pragmatic realism, not policy shifts.
